matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: MLH1 loss-of-function model (MLH1 mismatch-repair loss model)
MLH1 promoter methylation model (MLH1 epigenetic silencing model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MLH1 mismatch-repair review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context and Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed MLH1 loss-of-function model dosage suggests this sample carries the MLH1 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a MLH1 mismatch-repair review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context and Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as MLH1 mismatch-repair loss model. MLH1 loss-of-function model is curated as a MLH1 marker for Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched MLH1 loss-of-function model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the MLH1 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MLH1 mismatch-repair review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context and Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed MLH1 variant places this sample in the curated MLH1 research context. The local MLH1 bundle is mismatch-repair review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. MLH1 variants can alter or tag DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a MLH1 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MLH1 mismatch-repair review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context and Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability and colorectal/endometrial tumor biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. MLH1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local MLH1 bundle is mismatch-repair review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. MLH1 variants can alter or tag DNA mismatch repair, microsatellite stability, replication-error correction, Lynch syndrome biology, and promoter methylation context; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
